housing.summary: Athletic dorms at Georgia Tech are described as very nice with private rooms and living rooms. On-campus housing has security checkpoints for guests, though students find ways to bypass them. Limited direct discussion of general dorm quality in these posts.
key_quotes: The athletic dorms are very nice, each person gets their own room, has a living room, showers, etc.
I live on-campus in Atlanta so there are checkouts and security measures to guests in dorm. However, my roommate was able to bypass it and somehow sneak his girlfriend in.
She has been living with us like a 5th roommate since day one... using all the basic utilities she didn't pay for: shower, bathroom, kitchen

social_life.summary: Georgia Tech has a well-known reputation as a heavily male-skewed school, and students joke openly about the gender ratio. Social life struggles appear real - a founder literally paid students $10 to do 15-minute interviews about how hard it is to find people to do things with on campus. Alumni moving back to Atlanta suburbs post-graduation report feeling isolated and struggling to maintain social connections.
key_quotes: As the GT girls like to say, the odds are good, but the goods are odd.

I'm a founder going through CREATE-X doing customer discovery interviews to understand how GT students experience social life - how you find people to do stuff with, what's easy, what's frustrating, what you wish was different.
I moved back home after graduating to save money, and honestly don't really know anyone in my area anymore. I work remotely so I can't hang out with my coworkers irl.

dining.summary: There is virtually no discussion of dining hall quality or campus food options in these posts. The only food-adjacent mentions are jokes about eating peaches or drinking banana milk if accepted. This absence itself may be telling.
key_quotes: If I get into Georgia Tech I will literally eat 10 peaches and stream it live.
If I get into Georgia Tech I will drink 2 gallons of banana flavored milk in one sitting. It tastes like antibiotics.
Good thing Emory is just a car ride away

mental_health.summary: The academic grind at Georgia Tech is implicitly referenced repeatedly, with one student noting they 'ended up going where there was most prestige' and regretting it, drowning in schoolwork with no social life. The difficulty of the workload is treated as a given. Students with untreated mental health conditions like ADHD describe how it derailed their academic performance, and Georgia Tech's academic rigor leaves little margin for error.
key_quotes: I ended up going where there was most prestige, and I often wish I had picked a different school. All of my high school friends are loving their schools and I'm drowning in schoolwork and no social life.
People don't get how shitty untreated ADHD actually is to deal with academically and emotionally.

After I sort out my ADHD treatment and get meds, I'm going to go to community college for mathematics, get around a 4.0 GPA... Then I'll transfer to Georgia State, which I will then transfer to Georgia Tech.

financial_aid.summary: Georgia Tech's Stamps President's Scholar and Gold Scholar programs are mentioned as genuinely transformative, with one student calling it potentially 'a free ride.' The HOPE scholarship is a significant factor for Georgia residents. One student notes that Georgia Tech's financial aid offer was so superior to Northwestern's that it caused serious reconsideration of an ED commitment.
key_quotes: I was blown away when Georgia Tech released decisions and I got chosen as a Stamps President's Scholar/Gold Scholar semifinalist. This would mean I could potentially go to a school of this caliber for free.
ED is a scam to enhance a school's yield and rankings while controlling financial aid costs.
If I get around a 4.0 GPA, which will qualify me for the HOPE scholarship which pays full tuition and other grants that pay for other expenses.

academics.summary: Georgia Tech is consistently ranked in the top 5 for undergraduate CS alongside MIT, CMU, Stanford, and Berkeley, and is considered world-class for aerospace and other engineering disciplines. Career advising gets mixed reviews - students report the office gives everyone the same generic advice and it hasn't helped land internships. The workload is brutal enough that one student explicitly warned it destroyed their social life.
key_quotes: Every single time I've gone to get my resume checked, it gets hyped up like it's the best resume they've ever seen and yet I am unable to land even a single interview.
They give everyone the same advice. It's good advice but it's the same advice as the 50% of college students that go and seek more info.
Crime is not really an issue on the GT campus. Just don't be stupid or go off campus during the night.

overall_vibe.summary: Georgia Tech carries a strong reputation as a serious, grind-heavy engineering and tech school where prestige is real but social life takes a back seat. Students who get in are genuinely proud and excited - acceptance stories are emotionally charged. The school attracts highly motivated students but the culture leans heavily academic with the gender imbalance being a running joke even among prospective students.
key_quotes: I've been drowning in schoolwork and no social life.
It's been my dream school since I was 5.
Georgia Tech IS WHERE FRIEND'S DAUGHTER WANTS TO GO AND SHE DID THESE 1583940 ECS ONLY AS A FRESHMAN. WHAT DID YOU DO AS A FRESHMAN, HUH?

red_flags.summary: Campus cybersecurity is notably weak - students report receiving multiple phishing emails weekly from compromised student accounts, and even a Georgia Tech IT staff member acknowledged it as a known gap they're 'working on.' Crime concerns near campus surface occasionally, with one student noting you shouldn't go off campus at night. The career center appears to provide surface-level advice that hasn't translated to actual internship placements for many students.
key_quotes: It's ridiculous how many phishing emails I receive from random other students. There's no reason a student should be able to send mass emails to other students, let alone send a blatantly malicious email.
We're aware that sending phishing simulations to students is a gap we have today and we're working on that.
Just don't be stupid or go off campus during the night and you'll be fine.

hidden_gems.summary: The CREATE-X Startup Launch program is an active, funded entrepreneurship program running over the summer that supports student founders. The exchange program abroad is described as 'one of the best experiences in my life' and highly recommended. The transfer pathway through the Regents Engineering Transfer Pathway Program (RETP) is a legitimate and somewhat underknown route into Georgia Tech for students who didn't get in directly.
key_quotes: Having exchanged while at GT, it was one of the best experiences in my life. 1000% worth it if you like the country you're going to.
If you're dead set on transferring into GT, don't go to Georgia State. Check out the Regents Engineering Transfer Pathway Program.
I'm a founder going through CREATE-X Startup Launch this summer.
